observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:
1) Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRS), in accordance with Items 3.1 to 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011 for all the Cash in Bank accounts and submit the same to the Auditor within the timeline prescribed therein, to allow the prompt verification by the Audit Team and correction of the accounts balances in the books if any; and
2) 	Direct the Municipal Accounting Office to do the following:
Adjust the noted stale checks under APPENDIX G in accordance with Sections 59 & 61, Chapter 3, Volume I of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs to correct the balances of the affected Cash in Bank Accounts;
Correct the discrepancies noted in the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRSs) under APPENDIX H and the long-standing reported book reconciling items listed under APPENDIX I by verifying and analyzing the same to establish their existence, validity and accuracy;
Should adjusting entries be made, the same shall be drawn with sufficient supporting documents. A copy of the same shall be subsequently submitted to the Office of the Auditor for reference.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:
With Municipal Accounting Office shall prepare adjusting journal entries with working papers to bring into their correct balances the accounts misstated due to clerical error and submit a copy thereof to the Office of the Auditor; and
Henceforth, ensure that the Financial Statement balances are correct by double-checking and comparing the figures generated by the accounting system used and the manual excel file before finalizing the reports in order to produce financial information that are reliable and adequate.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:
Require the Municipal Accounting Office to consistently attach the supporting documents when recognizing year-end Accounts Payable in order for the Audit Team to effectively verify the validity and existence of the recognized obligations of the Agency; and
Immediately submit to the Office of the Auditor the supporting documents for the Accounts Payable amounting to P8,681,696.15 recognized in December 2024 in order for this office to verify the validity and existence thereof.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:
The Municipal Accounting Office personnel in-charge of recording deductions and processing remittances shall endeavor to prepare a reconciliation schedule with supporting documents for the variances noted under APPENDIX L of this AOM in order to have a basis for an updated and reconciled balance.
Should adjusting entries be necessary in view of the reconciliation schedule prepared, the same shall be drawn with sufficient supporting documents. A copy of the same shall be subsequently submitted to the resident auditor for reference;
The concerned personnel in charge of the DCPC and MEEO books shall see to it that the amounts under Due to PhilHealth are to be remitted on a monthly basis and prior to the scheduled remittance by the General Fund of PhilHealth contributions in order to avoid the utilization of cash under the General Fund to pay for PhilHealth remittances covering DCPC and MEEO contributions with no cash transferred yet. Additionally, the concerned personnel should ensure that the amount to be transferred on a monthly basis shall be exactly the amount of deductions for that specific month;
With regard to the unrecorded remittance showed in Table 1 of this AOM, the appropriate adjusting entry shall be made to record the same in the General Fund once the complete documents have been traced and forwarded to the Accounting Office; and
Henceforth, the concerned office shall endeavor to correctly record the deductions and remittances in the books of accounts to the end that the total amount recognized as Due to PhilHealh shall be the same amount recorded as remittance. Should there arise circumstances that would render the recording of deductions and remittances with variances, a reconciliation schedule shall be prepared showing the source and breakdown of the said variances.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:
Observation (a)
Direct the concerned offices to do exercise due diligence to ensure that withholding of tax are recorded timely accurately and remitted promptly to the BIR and to strictly adhere to Item III of BIR Revenue Memorandum Order No. 23-2014, Revenue Regulations (RR) No. 11-2018, National Internal Revenue Code (NIRC) and the other relevant rules and regulations promulgated by the Bureau of Internal Revenue to avoid penalties and surcharges, in addition to the tax thereon, that may become a personal liability of the persons responsible.
2) The Municipal Accounting Office to prepare the proposed adjusting entry for any excess withholding tax on compensation at year-end to reflect in the financial statements the correct outstanding liability of the Agency to the BIR.
Observation (b)
The Municipal Accounting Office personnel in-charge of recording withholding taxes and processing remittances shall endeavor to prepare a reconciliation schedule with supporting documents for the variances noted under ANNEX A of this AOM in order to have a basis for an updated and reconciled balance;
Should adjusting entries be necessary in view of the reconciliation schedule prepared, the same shall be drawn with sufficient supporting documents. A copy of the same shall be subsequently submitted to the resident auditor for reference;
Thereafter, should amounts of under remittances be determined after the year-end reconciliation, remit the same as soon as possible to avoid further penalties and possible legal issues. Moreover, initiate a coordination meeting between the various bookkeepers to reconcile the variances and schedule a monthly reconciliation of said account to avoid the same circumstance to occur in the future;
Further, the Municipal Accounting Office personnel in-charge of recording taxes withheld on compensation and processing remittances is advised to use one (1) Subsidiary Ledger for transactions involving withholding taxes on compensation to safeguard the account against inaccurate or misleading information and separate it from taxes withheld relative to transactions from suppliers.
Henceforth, the concerned office shall endeavor to correctly record the taxes withheld on compensation and remittances in the books of accounts to the end that the total amount recognized as Due to BIR shall be the same amount recorded as remittance. Should there arise circumstances that would render the recording of taxes withheld on compensation and remittances with variances, a reconciliation schedule shall be prepared showing the source and breakdown of the said variances.
Observation (c)
The concerned personnel in charge of the DCPC, MEEO and TF books shall see to it that the amounts under Due to BIR are to be remitted on a monthly basis and prior to the scheduled remittance by the General Fund of BIR Withholding Taxes in order to avoid the utilization of cash under the General Fund to pay for BIR remittances covering other funds with no cash transferred yet. Additionally, the concerned personnel should ensure that the amount to be transferred on a monthly basis shall be exactly the amount of deductions for that specific month.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:
1. Secure a copy of DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2016, be knowledgeable of the procedures stated therein and among others, consistently adhere to the prescribed rates of Hazard Pay to PHWs under Item 3.3 thereof and likewise comply with the provisions of Item 3.6 on the non-entitlement to Hazard Pay of PHWs who have a combined leave and/or full time attendance in training, seminars et al. totaling at least eleven (11) working days in a month, otherwise, any excess Hazard Pay shall be subject to suspension or disallowance, as the case may be;
2. Secure a copy of DBM-DSWD Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2018, be knowledgeable of the procedures stated therein and among others, consistently adhere to the prescribed formula in computing Hazard Pay for PSWs under Item 5.3.1 thereof and likewise comply with the provisions of Item 5.3.2 on the non-entitlement to Hazard Pay of PSWs for those days which they are on  leave of absence and/or attending training, seminars, et al., otherwise, any excess Hazard Pay shall be subject to suspension or disallowance, as the case may be
3. Henceforth, adhere to the documentary requirements required in Item 5.8 of COA Circular 2012-001 for the payment of Hazard Pay in order to readily facilitate the audit and validation on the grant of same and in order to guide the personnel in-charge in computing the proper amount of said benefit; and
4. Likewise, consistently attach a copy of Travel Orders and Certificates of Appearance for working days which are utilized for Official Travel in order for the concerned offices to verify the nature of the said travel which shall be a factor in determining the amount of Hazard Pay due.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:
Submit the monthly Reports on Utilization of DRRMF for CY 2024, duly certified correct by the Municipal Accountant, to the Audit Team and henceforth observe timely submission of the said report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month; and
Henceforth, require the LDRRMO to prepare the LDRRMFIP using the prescribed format required by COA Circular No. 2012-002 as shown in APPENDIX S of this report in order for the Municipality’s reports to be in line with the aforementioned circular and to be able to show information relevant to the intended users.

We recommended to management to adopt the following measures:

The Municipal Accounting Office is to draw an Adjusting Journal Entry supported with proper and complete documents, to drop the amount of the donated land to the PNP from the books of accounts;
 
The items in the Land account which are in the nature of land improvements and structures/buildings are to be reclassified to their proper accounts through the drawing of Adjusting Journal Entries supported with proper and complete documents; and 

The Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal Assessors Office shall continuously coordinate so that an updated and reconciled report on land owned by the Municipality can be presented in the Financial Statements and provided to end-users and stakeholders.
observations.managementAction: Conducted One-time Cleansing of PPEs in CY 2024.
observations.reason: Subject land was still included in PPE balance as of year-end.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:

Adopt the Accrual Method of recording School Fees pertaining to the Free Higher Education Program by recognizing the same upon the transmittal and receipt by CHED of the billing statement for each semester as shown in the suggested journal entries in Annex F.
The Accounting Office and the DCPC shall consistently coordinate with each other to the end that the former is immediately furnished with a copy of the billing statements being transmitted to and received by CHED to enable timely, detailed and proper recording.

observations.reason: A portion of Recommendation No. 2 is yet to be implemented.
Long-standing reconciling items were not adjusted in the books.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:

Require the Municipal Accounting Office to consistently observe the Accrual Basis of Accounting as prescribed by the NGAS Manual for LGUs in recording transactions relating Fidelity Bond and Insurance Premiums; and

If deemed practicable, draw the adjusting Journal Entries for the unexpired portion of the Fidelity Bond and Insurance Premiums supported with working papers, in order to reflect the correct amounts of the related prepayments in the books of accounts of the agency.

We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:

The Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office shall coordinate with each other to the end that the former is given a copy of the loan documents sufficient enough for it to disclose the necessary information of the Loans Payable account in the Notes to Financial Statements; and

Submit to this office the schedule of Loans Payable - Domestic showing the following details as of December 31, 2023: name of the creditor, principal amount, terms of the loan, purpose of the loan and the loan status.

We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:

The Municipal Accounting Office shall henceforth disclose the amount and details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in accordance with the format prescribed in COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.reason: Details of LDRRMF were still not discussed in the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:

Require the concerned department/unit heads to submit complete PPMPs inclusive of all projects, be it goods, services or civil works to be procured, in precedence to the preparation of the APP by the BAC Secretariat for consolidation; 

Require the BAC Secretariat to prepare consolidated APP in view of the submitted PPMPs wherein such APP is inclusive of all projects, be it goods, services or civil works and which is likewise reconciled with the proposed annual budget; and

Stop the practice of preparing separate APP and PPMP per infrastructure/civil work project and ensure that no procurement is made if the same is not in the approved and consolidated APP.

We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:

Submit to this office a copy of the DepEd-approved SIP and DEDP for the purpose of verifying the compliance of the CY 2023 SEF Budget to Sec. 4.1 and 5.2 of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, Series of 2017;

Henceforth, consistently adhere with the guidelines prescribed in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, Series of 2017 with regard to the Planning, Budgeting and Utilization of the SEF to ensure that all expenditure items to be included in the SEF Budget shall be among those provided under the aforementioned Joint Circular and contained in the approved SIP and DEDP and further, that all items chargeable against the SEF shall be net of the budgetary provision for the same or related item(s) in the budget for the DepEd and the ECCD Council, and that which may be funded out of the SEF; and

Ensure that only the priority and allowable disbursements are charged against the SEF so as not to deplete the funds for purposes other than for which it was created.

We recommended to management to adopt the following measures:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to use the Municipal Treasurer’s Office’s certified list of taxpayers showing the amount due for the year in setting up the Real Property Tax/Special Education Tax receivable and deferred income at the beginning of the year as required under Section 20, Volume 1 of NGAS Manual;

Draw Journal Entry Voucher to bring into harmony the aforementioned balances reflected in the Financial Statements and the Summary of Delinquent Taxes; and

Require reconciliation between the Municipal Assessor’s Office’s Report on Real Property Assessment and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office’s certified list of taxpayers showing the amount due for the year before submitting the same to the Municipal Accounting Office as basis for setting up the RPT and SET Receivable and deferred income at the beginning of every year.

We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:

Apply the provisions of NGAS for LGUs which is to record procured goods/supplies initially as assets, except those purchased out of the Petty Cash Fund. Prepare the SSMI which shall be done weekly and shall serve as basis of the Mun. Accounting Office in recording the inventory expenses as they take place;

Exert effort to conduct physical inventory count semi-annually and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) and submit the same to the Auditor concerned not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively; and

3.  Expedite the creation Inventory Committee who shall then be the spearhead in conducting physical count of inventories and other properties owned by the LGU.
observations.reason: Reports on the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) as of June 30, 2024 and December 31, 2024 was not submitted to the Audit Team.
Pending drawing of adjusting entries in view of change in accounting policy.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to management to adopt the following measures:
The Municipal Accounting Office is to draw an Adjusting Journal Entry supported with proper and complete documents, to drop the amount of the donated land to the PNP from the books of accounts;
The items in the Land account which are in the nature of land improvements and structures/buildings are to be reclassified to their proper accounts through the drawing of Adjusting Journal Entries supported with proper and complete documents; and
The Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal Assessors Office shall continuously coordinate so that an updated and reconciled report on land owned by the Municipality can be presented in the Financial Statements and provided to end-users and stakeholders.

We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:
Adopt the Accrual Method of recording School Fees pertaining to the Free Higher Education Program by recognizing the same upon the transmittal and receipt by CHED of the billing statement for each semester as shown in the suggested journal entries below:
Sample entries for 1st Semester SY 2024-2025
>Transmittal to and receipt by CHED in September 2024 of the Billing Statement from DCPC amounting to P30,000,000.00 based on the list of enrollees for the aforementioned semester:
100% of billed amount is approved
>Receipt by DCPC of the the billed amount in December 2023, granting 100% of the billed amount is approved:
>Journal Entry in January 2025 to reverse Deferred Revenue - School Fees and recognize Revenue attributable to CY 2025.
Less than 100% of billed amount is approved
>Receipt by DCPC of the the billed amount in December 2023, with less than 100% of the billed amount is approved:
>Journal Entry in January 2025 to reverse Deffered Revenue - School Fees and recognize Revenue attributable to CY 2025.
The Accounting Office and the DCPC shall consistently coordinate with each other to the end that the former is immediately furnished with a copy of the billing statements being transmitted to and received by CHED to enable timely, detailed and proper recording.

We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:
1) Require the Municipal Accounting Office to consistently observe the Accrual Basis of Accounting as prescribed by the NGAS Manual for LGUs in recording transactions relating Fidelity Bond and Insurance Premiums; and
If deemed practicable, draw the adjusting Journal Entries for the unexpired portion of the Fidelity Bond and Insurance Premiums supported with working papers, in order to reflect the correct amounts of the related prepayments in the books of accounts of the agency.

We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:
The Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal Treasurer’s Office shall coordinate with each other to the end that the former is given a copy of the loan documents sufficient enough for it to disclose the necessary information of the Loans Payable account in the Notes to Financial Statements; and
Submit to this office the schedule of Loans Payable - Domestic showing the following details as of December 31, 2023: name of the creditor, principal amount, terms of the loan, purpose of the loan and the loan status.

We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:
The Municipal Accounting Office shall henceforth disclose the amount and details of     the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in accordance with the format prescribed in  COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:
Require the concerned department/unit heads to submit complete PPMPs inclusive of all projects, be it goods, services or civil works to be procured, in precedence to the preparation of the APP by the BAC Secretariat for consolidation;
Require the BAC Secretariat to prepare consolidated APP in view of the submitted PPMPs wherein such APP is inclusive of all projects, be it goods, services or civil works and which is likewise reconciled with the proposed annual budget; and
Stop the practice of preparing separate APP and PPMP per infrastructure/civil work project and ensure that no procurement is made if the same is not in the approved and consolidated APP.

We recommended to the management to adopt the following measures:
Submit to this office a copy of the DepEd-approved SIP and DEDP for the purpose of verifying the compliance of the CY 2023 SEF Budget to Sec. 4.1 and 5.2 of DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, Series of 2017;
Henceforth, consistently adhere with the guidelines prescribed in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, Series of 2017 with regard to the Planning, Budgeting and Utilization of the SEF to ensure that all expenditure items to be included in the SEF Budget shall be among those provided under the aforementioned Joint Circular and contained in the approved SIP and DEDP and further, that all items chargeable against the SEF shall be net of the budgetary provision for the same or related item(s) in the budget for the DepEd and the ECCD Council, and that which may be funded out of the SEF; and
Ensure that only the priority and allowable disbursements are charged against the SEF so as not to deplete the funds for purposes other than for which it was created.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to evaluate the Schedule of Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds account and determine which items need to be immediately settled or which require the necessary journal entries in order for them to be eliminated from the reciprocal/intra-agency accounts for the purpose of enhancing the fair presentation of accounts in the consolidated financial statements; and
Evaluate the implementation of projects for which fund transfers were made and draw the adjusting entries if the same were completely implemented. Otherwise, facilitate the completion of documents needed for request for write-off of dormant unliquidated fund transfers from COA pursuant to COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

Observe the requirements of the abovementioned circulars by establishing procedures and mechanism which will ensure that the cash pertaining to the unexpended balance of the MDRRMF is actually transferred to the Trust Fund at year-end. Moreover, the amount and details of the said funds should henceforth be disclosed in the Notes to the Financial Statements; and
Ensure that the said fund shall be utilized for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction and management activities as required by laws, rules and regulations.  The accrued amount shall be used solely for the purpose of supporting disaster risk reduction management activities within the next five years.
observations.reason: Disclosure Recommendation Reiterated in CY 2023 AAR
Physical Count not yet conducted pending One-Time Cleansing
Pending drawing of adjusting entries in view of change in accoun ting policy.

observations.recommendation:
1) Submit to this office a legal basis of the Economic Assistance granted for CY 2022 for validation as to the legality and validity of the subject transaction and for the audit team to determine the appropriate action with regard to the result of the validation; and
2) Refrain from granting and committing to grant benefits and/or incentives when there is no legal basis to back up the same in order to avoid possible suspensions and/or disallowances of the transaction and/or refunds from the recipients considering that the said circumstance could cause undue burden on the concerned payees.
observations.reason: Implemented as to submission of legal basis (for Audit Evaluation)

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the management to instruct the Department Heads concerned to initiate the transfer of the balance amount of P16, 712,591.47 from its Trust Fund books to General Fund Proper and Municipal Economic Enterprise books of accounts in compliance with the aforementioned regulations and enacted ordinance. When transferred, this amount is eligible for appropriation during the ensuing year.

We recommended to the Management to require the following offices:
The Municipal Accounting Office to:
Submit existing documents, as claimed, to support the accounting entries made recognizing Accounts Payable and Fuel, Oil and Lubricants Expense in the total amount of P5,298,905.00, and draw necessary adjusting entries if warranted to reflect the accurate balances of affected accounts.
Adhere to IPSAS 19 and provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009, and discontinue the recording of accounts payable for the value of goods that have not been received or are not yet due and demandable. Hence, disclose the same in the Notes to Financial Statements following Section 3.5 of COA Accounting Circular No. 2007-02 dated January 19, 2007.
Coordinate with the Municipal Budget Office and with the concerned offices of the Municipality on the obligations not yet due and demandable and on the submission of reports supporting the actual delivery of goods, respectively, so as to facilitate the correct recording of Accounts Payable in the books of accounts.
Adhere to IPSAS 1 and provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-009, and discontinue the recording of fuel, oil, and lubricants Expense without actual utilization of goods.
Coordinate with the concerned offices of the Municipality and establish a monthly cut-off period for the submission before the closing date of the receipt and utilization reports on fuel, oil, and lubricants to facilitate the accurate recording of Fuel, Oil, and Lubricant Expense in the books of accounts.
The Municipal Budget Office to:
Prepare a list of not yet due and demandable obligations following Section 3.5 of COA Accounting Circular No. 2007-02 dated January 19, 2007, and coordinate with the Municipal Accounting Office with regards the same to facilitate reconciliation at the end of the year.
All of the Concerned Offices/Departments to:
Submit the receipt and utilization reports on fuel, oil, and lubricants, agree and coordinate with the Municipal Accounting Office the monthly cut-off period before the closing date for the subsequent submissions of the same reports.

We recommended to the Management to specifically require the following offices/units responsible:
The Municipal Accounting Office to:
Consistently adopt the perpetual inventory method in recording purchases of supplies and materials for stock and record issuances of the same to end-users following Section 114, Chapter 7, Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGUs, and
Subsequently prepare journal entry vouchers to record inventory issuances on the basis of the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and supporting Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) as required in Section 122, Chapter 7, Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGUs.
The Municipal Treasurer’s Office to:
Consolidate Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS), prepare the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) and forward the same to the Municipal Accounting Office to facilitate the recording of inventory issuances in the corresponding expense accounts as required under Section 122, Chapter 7, Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGUs.
The Supply Officers and Inventory Committee to:
Conduct the periodic physical inventory of supplies and materials semi-annually and prepare the Report on Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) as required in Section 114, Chapter 7, Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGUs.

We recommended to the HRMO and Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of the required supporting documents on the monthly claim of salaries, RATA and PERA of the Sanggunian Bayan Members and staff.  Moreover, require the submission of the lacking documentary requirements for the foregoing mentioned period.  Unless otherwise they are submitted, the condition affected the accuracy of the earned leave balances of concerned personnel.

We recommended to the Management to:
Refrain from paying to its officials and employees those allowances, incentives and other fringe benefits not covered by existing appropriations and, consequently, have no legal basis.  Further payments may lead to audit disallowances as provided under COA Circular No. 2013-003 dated January 30, 2013; and
Follow the pertinent provision of DBM Budget Circular No. 2006-1 dated February 1, 2006 in the granting of rice subsidy to personnel.

We recommended to the management to refrain from charging the payments for the services rendered by individuals hired through contracts of services against Honoraria and to charge it against the MOOE instead in accordance with COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2, s. 2020.

We recommended to the management to do the following:
Advise the payroll personnel to follow the rules under Section 1, Rule VI of R.A. No. 9679 for purposes of properly computing the government share as well as personal share of the HDMF contributions.
Advice the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds to cover employer’s contribution due the HMDF based on the maximum monthly compensation of P5,000.00, otherwise the computation of allowable PS limitation will be adversely affected.

We recommended to the management to:
Require the End-User Units to prepare the PPMPs in accordance with Section 7 of RA 9184 using the suggested format by the GPPB duly posted in its website; and
Consolidate the PPMPs and prepare the APP in accordance to the applicable provisions of RA 9184 using the prescribed format under GPPB Circular No. 07-2015 and GPPB Resolution No. 20-2015 dated July 16, 2015 through the BAC Secretariat (copies of the GPPB circular and resolution are herein attached).

We recommended to the management to require the concerned Project Engineer to duly submit liquidation report such as Report of Production of the 10,940 cubic meters (1,100 loads) in compliance to Government Gratuity Permit and to account the 1,100 gratuity coupons to determine actual delivery of these filling materials to its destination, the specific municipal roads and canals maintained.

We recommended requiring the BAC to justify the obtaining condition for further evaluation by this Office and require the winning bidder to submit Supply Contract and related documents evidencing payments of imposed levy due the Provincial Government of Bukidnon.

We recommended to the management, particularly the Municipal Accounting Office, to exert more effort to reconcile the outstanding balance of Loans Payable to reflect the correct balance of the account presented in the Financial Statements as of December 31, 2020; reduce the same in a working paper showing all the details and information needed; and with supporting documents. Thereafter, the Muncipal Accounting Office to make adjusting entries on the appropriate funds affected.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry after completely substantiated with proper documents in order to record a receivable, Due from Other Funds and a liability, Trust Liabilities – DRRM, in the Special Trust Fund books amounting to P5,521,827.81.  For subsequent transfer of the unutilized disaster fund, use Due to Other Funds account, instead of Other Payable in the General Fund.

We recommended to the Management to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries after completely substantiated with proper documents in order to correct the overstated amounts of the affected accounts.  Further, department Heads must always coordinate with each other as to their assigned tasks in order to generate very accurate and reliable financial statements.

We further recommended to the management, particularly the Municipal Accounting Office, to record only certified payables: like services already rendered or goods already delivered, with complete supporting documents every end of each year.

We recommended requiring the Municipal Accountant to make an adjusting entry to correct the overstated amounts of the affected accounts in the ensuing year and to refrain from booking obligations based on certified list of obligations only.  For similar succeeding transactions, require also the Municipal Budget Officer to consider as LGU’s commitment /obligation only those transactions which actually are delivered, billed and rendered as not to classify the account as ghost payable.  For those transactions that are fully obligated but not yet delivered or billed as of the year end, they shall be reported in the SCBAA only.

We recommended to the Management to require the concerned Municipal Officials to submit the mentioned documents for further review by this Office. In so doing, audit decision in the form of audit suspension and/or audit disallowance maybe imposed when appropriate, to affected payee.

We recommended to the management to require the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to initiate the return of P15, 295,701.80 from 20% MDF to General Fund Proper representing the appropriated amount for continuing appropriation to purchase different capital outlays.  Require also the Municipal Budget Officer to furnish the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer the pertinent SAAOb for continuing appropriation under 20% MDF as their guide in proper recording in the books of accounts and check/cash payments, respectively.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Treasurer to substantiate the transfer made by submitting the related bank statements, debit/credit advices together with the approval of the Municipal Mayor in order to establish the validity, existence and correctness of the said transfer of funds. Henceforth, submit them to the Office of the Auditor for her further verification and review.

We recommended to the management to require all the BAC Members and the Technical Working Group to revisit and strictly observe RA 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations and to submit an explanation on the noted deficiencies mentioned in this AOM. Also, necessary trainings and orientations related to procurement laws, rules and regulations must be continuously provided to the staffs and employees assigned at the BAC office.

We recommended to the management to direct the BAC to require all winning bidders of the goods and services and infrastructure projects to submit Performance Securities within the prescribed period and to enforce sanctions provided in Section 40 of the IRR-A.

We recommended to the management to strictly implement the provisions mentioned under COA Circular No. 2009-001 and direct the personnel-in-charge to render extra efforts with the release of contracts, together with all the supporting documents, to furnish to the Commission on Audit, within five (5) working days upon issuance.

We recommended to the management, particularly the BAC, to revisit the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR regarding the required timeline in the procurement activities in order to expedite the process and avoid penalties under Rule XXI of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and schedule necessary orientations, trainings and workshops for the procurement processes and activities for the BAC staffs and employees.

We recommended that Management for subsequent similar transactions will require the end user of the Infrastructure Projects whose labor component is more than P500, 000.00 to implement the same by straight contract.  If the former insisted their implementation by administration and there is no existing pakyaw groups in the locality, appoint a Project Facilitator (PF) who will assist in organizing Pakyaw workers into groups based on the lump sum works or per scope of works example painting, masonry, electrical, carpentry, steel, stone works etc.

We recommended to the management to submit to the Office of the Auditor an explanation or justification on the deficiencies noted. 

Further, we recommended to the COVID-19 Procuring Unit to review whether or not minimum documentary requirements are duly complied before recommending award for the HOPE’s approval and subsequently, the issuance of Notice to Proceed and to follow diligently the governing procurement process during the pandemic period.

We recommended to the management for the submission of the following documents in order to complete the post audit activity of the Auditor on the said transactions:

1.	 Details of the goods purchased as to specific name of the product, size and weight; and
2.	Inspection and Acceptance Report indicating the details of the goods including the quantity or number of pieces per item.

Further, we recommended to the management the following course(s) of action for all succeeding similar transactions:

1.	 Properly fill-up documents such as Purchase Requests, Requests for Quotation and Purchase Orders in accordance with its official design and certified/approved by proper agency officials;
2.	Review the data indicated in the Purchase Request to ascertain its correctness and specificity;
3.	Include the brand names together with the specifications of the items quoted by the supplier in response to the advertised relevant characteristics and or/performance requirements of the items needed by the agency in the Purchase Orders to serve as reference during deliveries, inspection and acceptance by management; and
4.	Management should exercise preference on locally-manufactured products or brands over foreign ones when time is of the essence or during critical situations.

We recommended to the Management to ask for the Certified List of Beneficiaries from the DSWD so that the Audit Team could validate existence/legitimacy of the reported recipients and validity, legality and propriety of the payments could be established.

We further recommended to the management that thereafter, to observe religiously specific guidelines in implementing programs funded by national offices in order to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Treasurer to timely prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report on Unseviciable Property (IIURP) to report all unserviciable and obsolete items previously taken uo in the books as assets so that they may be properly monitored, disposed of and dropped from the accounts.

Also, we recommended to the management to create a disposal committee to conduct inspection/ inventory of disposal assets and determine its appraised value and the mode of disposal in accordance with the existing guidelines on supply and property management.

We recommended to the management, particularly the Municipal Accounting Office, to exert more effort to reconcile the outstanding balance of Loans Payable to reflect the correct balance of the account presented in the Financial Statements as of December 31, 2020; reduce the same in a working paper showing all the details and information needed; and with supporting documents. Thereafter, the Muncipal Accounting Office to make adjusting entries on the appropriate funds affected.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare an adjusting entry after completely substantiated with proper documents in order to record a receivable, Due from Other Funds and a liability, Trust Liabilities – DRRM, in the Special Trust Fund books amounting to P5,521,827.81.  For subsequent transfer of the unutilized disaster fund, use Due to Other Funds account, instead of Other Payable in the General Fund.

The Municipal Accountant must also insure that reciprocal accounts are reconciled at the end of the year.  Further, the accounting personnel must always coordinate with each other as to their assigned accounting tasks in order to generate very accurate and reliable financial statements.

We recommended to the Management to require the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries after completely substantiated with proper documents in order to correct the overstated amounts of the affected accounts.  Further, department Heads must always coordinate with each other as to their assigned tasks in order to generate very accurate and reliable financial statements.

We further recommended to the management, particularly the Municipal Accounting Office, to record only certified payables: like services already rendered or goods already delivered, with complete supporting documents every end of each year.

We recommended requiring the Municipal Accountant to make an adjusting entry to correct the overstated amounts of the affected accounts in the ensuing year and to refrain from booking obligations based on certified list of obligations only.  For similar succeeding transactions, require also the Municipal Budget Officer to consider as LGU’s commitment /obligation only those transactions which actually are delivered, billed and rendered as not to classify the account as ghost payable.  For those transactions that are fully obligated but not yet delivered or billed as of the year end, they shall be reported in the SCBAA only.

We recommended to the Management to require the concerned Municipal Officials to submit the mentioned documents for further review by this Office. In so doing, audit decision in the form of audit suspension and/or audit disallowance maybe imposed when appropriate, to affected payee.

We recommended to the management to require the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to initiate the return of P15, 295,701.80 from 20% MDF to General Fund Proper representing the appropriated amount for continuing appropriation to purchase different capital outlays.  Require also the Municipal Budget Officer to furnish the Offices of the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer the pertinent SAAOb for continuing appropriation under 20% MDF as their guide in proper recording in the books of accounts and check/cash payments, respectively.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Treasurer to substantiate the transfer made by submitting the related bank statements, debit/credit advices together with the approval of the Municipal Mayor in order to establish the validity, existence and correctness of the said transfer of funds. Henceforth, submit them to the Office of the Auditor for her further verification and review.

We recommeded to the management to require all the BAC Members and the Technical Working Group to revisit and strictly observe RA 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations and to submit an explanation on the noted deficiencies mentioned in this AOM. Also, necessary trainings and orientations related to procurement laws, rules and regulations must be continuously provided to the staffs and employees assigned at the BAC office.

We recommended to the management to direct the BAC to require all winning bidders of the goods and services and infrastructure projects to submit Performance Securities within the prescribed period and to enforce sanctions provided in Section 40 of the IRR-A.

We recommended to the management to strictly implement the provisions mentioned under COA Circular No. 2009-001 and direct the personnel-in-charge to render extra efforts with the release of contracts, together with all the supporting documents, to furnish to the Commission on Audit, within five (5) working days upon issuance.

We recommended to the management, particularly the BAC, to revisit the provisions of RA 9184 and its IRR regarding the required timeline in the procurement activities in order to expedite the process and avoid penalties under Rule XXI of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 and schedule necessary orientations, trainings and workshops for the procurement processes and activities for the BAC staffs and employees.

We recommended that Management for subsequent similar transactions will require the end user of the Infrastructure Projects whose labor component is more than P500, 000.00 to implement the same by straight contract.  If the former insisted their implementation by administration and there is no existing pakyaw groups in the locality, appoint a Project Facilitator (PF) who will assist in organizing Pakyaw workers into groups based on the lump sum works or per scope of works example painting, masonry, electrical, carpentry, steel, stone works etc.

We recommended to the management to submit to the Office of the Auditor an explanation or justification on the deficiencies noted.

Further, we recommended to the COVID-19 Procuring Unit to review whether or not minimum documentary requirements are duly complied before recommending award for the HOPE’s approval and subsequently, the issuance of Notice to Proceed and to follow diligently the governing procurement process during the pandemic period.

We recommended to the management for the submission of the following documents in order to complete the post audit activity of the Auditor on the said transactions:
Details of the goods purchased as to specific name of the product, size and weight; and
Inspection and Acceptance Report indicating the details of the goods including the quantity or number of pieces per item.

Further, we recommended to the management the following course(s) of action for all succeeding similar transactions:
Properly fill-up documents such as Purchase Requests, Requests for Quotation and Purchase Orders in accordance with its official design and certified/approved by proper agency officials;
Review the data indicated in the Purchase Request to ascertain its correctness and specificity;
Include the brand names together with the specifications of the items quoted by the supplier in response to the advertised relevant characteristics and or/performance requirements of the items needed by the agency in the Purchase Orders to serve as reference during deliveries, inspection and acceptance by management; and
Management should exercise preference on locally-manufactured products or brands over foreign ones when time is of the essence or during critical situations.

We recommended to the Management to ask for the Certified List of Beneficiaries from the DSWD so that the Audit Team could validate existence/legitimacy of the reported recipients and validity, legality and propriety of the payments could be established.

We further recommended to the management that thereafter, to observe religiously specific guidelines in implementing programs funded by national offices in order to avoid suspension or disallowance in audit.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Accountant to coordinate with Municipal Engineer for proper documentation of the completed projects of the Municipality as the Accountant’s basis in the reclassification entry for the Construction in Progress –Infrastructure Assets account to the appropriate asset accounts and in the computation and recording of the corresponding annual depreciation for fair presentation in the Financial Statements. Also, require the Accountant to adjust the CIP-Infrastructure Assets account to their appropriate asset account and other affected accounts as follows:

Infrastructure Assets 36,778,123.02
Government Equity    8,725,946.99
     Construction in Progress  36,778,123.02
     Accumulated Depreciation  8,725,946.99

We recommended to direct the Municipal Accountant to align the method used in recording of the borrowing costs with the guidelines on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% of the Annual Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for the Municipality’s development projects set forth under Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017 – 1 dated February 22, 2017 and perform adjusting entries (as stated below) following the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) 5 Volume I (Borrowing Cost—Allowed Alternative Treatment) so as to fairly reflect the balances of the accounts in the Financial Statements. Disclose also the method used in recording the borrowing costs in the year-end Financial Statements. 

Construction in Progress –Buildings and Other Structures     16,631,851.54
         Government Equity 16,631,851.54

We recommended to the management to direct the Municipal Accountant to trace and determine the unreconciled Construction in Progress- Infrastructure Assets balance between the General Ledger and its supporting schedules to ascertain the balances of the account as reflected in the Financial Statements as of December 31, 2019.

We recommended to the management, that every start of the year, to require the Municipal Assessor to furnish the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant the Report on Real Property Assessment as the basis in recording the taxable Real Property, accruing for both General and Special Education Funds.  Also, the Municipal Treasurer’s and Accounting Offices arrive at the correct real property tax collectible balance so that the same could be reflected in the financial statements of the municipality.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Treasurer to diligently monitor that collections should be cut-off at the end of the month to report collections made for the period.  Subsequently, prepare collection reports for their submission to the Municipal Accountant’s Office. He latter should demand complete submission of collection reports from the former to properly account collections made either remitted or not remitted during the year. Otherwise, the condition does not follow with the consistency principle of accounting and resulted to understatement of Cash, Revenue and Liability accounts presented in the financial statements as of the end of the year.

We recommended to the management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to recognize accrued interest receivable and interest income accounts. Set up subsidiary record for interest earned on each savings account for the period to facilitate transfer thereof to General Fund Proper in order to enhance the monitoring of interest earned and its balances.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Accountant to review the details and status of the balances to determine the appropriate action to be done.  The write-off of balances may be recommended by the Accountant if warranted   subject to prescribed procedures, rules, and regulations of COA.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Treasurer to timely prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Property (IIURP) to report all unserviceable and obsolete items previously taken up in the books as assets so that they may be properly monitored, disposed of and dropped from the accounts. 

Also, we recommended to the management to create a disposal committee to conduct inspection/inventory of disposable assets and determine its appraised value and the mode of disposal in accordance with the existing guidelines on supply and property management.

We recommended to the management to review the budget as well as the Programs, Activities and Projects (P/A/Ps) of the Municipality to align with the requirements stated under Section 7 (b) of RA No. 10410, otherwise known as the "Early Years Act of 2013" which provides for the allocations of SEF for the ECCD Program so that the intended beneficiaries/school children can enjoy the benefits and their educational standards will be uplifted.

We recommended to the management to strictly implement the provisions mentioned under COA Circular No. 2009-001 and direct the personnel-in-charge to render extra efforts with the release of contracts to furnish to the Commission on Audit, within five (5) working days upon issuance.

We recommended to the management to immediately discontinue the disbursements of the said Rice Allowances to the officials and employees of the Municipality and adhere strictly to specific statutory authority in the appropriation of fund as reviewed by the Provincial Local Finance Committee and include only on the priorities the grant of personnel compensation benefits expressly authorized by law.

We recommended to the management to set-up “Due to Other Funds” account in the GF Proper to establish liability from other funds in the event time is the essence to cope with the deadline set by the BIR and when there is still no actual cash transferred yet from other funds.

We recommended to the management to submit the following documents in order to facilitate continuance of our audit and validate that the Municipality have met all the CSC Memorandum Circular requirements warranting the grant of the monetary awards:

a.	Detailed Employee Suggestions and Incentive Awards System
b.	Documents evidencing the generation of savings due to any extraordinary contribution or exceptional accomplishment by an employee which resulted in such monetary savings. In case savings are generated, monetary awards shall be granted which shall not exceed 20% thereof.
c. 	Approved Program on Awards and Incentives for Service Excellence (PRAISE) Model by the Civil Service Commission (CSC)

We recommended to the management to direct the personnel-in-charge with the submission of the required documents as mentioned under COA Circular 2012-001 and submit such other documents deemed necessary for the auditorial review and in the technical evaluation hereof to the Commission on Audit. Also, we recommended to the management to follow the provisions under Executive Order No. 1035 for the expeditious acquisition of the real property for the Infrastructure Project and other government development projects.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Engineer and Other Officers concerned to:

1. 	 Reassess the viability of PPAs funded under the 20% Development Fund, and fast-tract its implementation to warrant delivery of benefits to the intended beneficiaries.

2. 	 If projects were no longer feasible then revert the funds to unappropriated surplus under the 20% Development Fund.

We recommended that management to direct the personnel-in-charge with the submission of the required documents as mentioned under COA Circular 2012-001 and submit such other documents deemed necessary for the auditorial review and in the technical evaluation hereof to the Commission on Audit. Also, we recommend to follow the provisions under Executive Order No. 1035 for the expeditious acquisition of the real property for the Infrastructure Project and other government development projects.

We recommended to the management to require the Municipal Engineer and Other Officers concerned to:
Reassess the viability of PPAs funded under the 20% Development Fund, and fast-tract its implementation to warrant delivery of benefits to the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that the unobligated lump sum appropriations of P10, 535,745.21 be itemized or identified as to their specific locations.  Such itemized appropriations shall be for specific development projects, programs and activities embodied in the approved local development plan formulated and prioritized by the Local Development Council. Thereafter seek approval from the Sanggunian before any disbursements be made pursuant to above stated provision of law.  In so doing all developmental projects are duly accounted and listed in the books of accounts.

Moreover, direct the Municipal Accountant to trace and determine the unreconciled Construction in Progress- Infrastructure Assets balance between the General Ledger and its supporting schedules to ascertain the balances of the account as reflected in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should timely prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Property (IIURP) to report all unserviceable and obsolete items previously taken up in the books as assets so that they may be properly monitored, disposed of and dropped from the accounts. In addition, Municipal Accountant should reclassify the unserviceable properties to Other Assets account.

Also, we recommended that management creates a disposal committee to conduct inspection/inventory of disposable assets and determine its appraised value and the mode of disposal in accordance with the existing guidelines on supply and property management.

We recommended to instruct the Municipal Accountant to recognize accrued interest payable at year-end and set-up subsidiary record for each loan accounts in order to enhance the monitoring of loan payments and the balances.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to follow the afore-cited provision. Regularly review the unexpended LDRRMF which are already more than five (5) years in the special trust fund. Consequently, the pertinent amounts should be reverted back to the general fund of the municipality following the afore-cited entries so that it can be utilized by making it available for other social services as stipulated therein.

We recommended that Management should take necessary legal action to recover the 6.75% or P 13,021,780.78 variance between the contract cost and COA evaluated cost from the concerned Contractor whenever the opinion of COA Regional Office is sustained by the General Counsel

We recommended that management strictly implement the provisions mentioned under COA Circular No. 2009-001 and direct the personnel-in-charge to render extra efforts with the release of contracts to furnish to the Commission on Audit, within five (5) working days upon issuance.

We recommended to require the Municipal Engineer and Other Officers concerned for proper prioritization of fully funded projects to ensure their completion on its target set, thus precluding unwarranted delay of the implementation of programs, projects and activities.
We recommended that Management should undertake possible measures to expedite the full implementation and total completion of this project.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the unobligated lump sum appropriations of P10, 535,745.21 be itemized or identified as to their specific locations.  Such itemized appropriations shall be for specific development projects, programs and activities embodied in the approved local development plan formulated and prioritized by the Local Development Council. Thereafter seek approval from the Sanggunian before any disbursements be made pursuant to above stated provision of law.  In so doing all developmental projects are duly accounted and listed in the books of accounts.
Moreover, direct the Municipal Accountant to trace and determine the unreconciled Construction in Progress- Infrastructure Assets balance between the General Ledger and its supporting schedules to ascertain the balances of the account as reflected in the Financial Statements.

Also, we recommended that management creates a disposal committee to conduct inspection/inventory of disposable assets and determine its appraised value and the mode of disposal in accordance with the existing guidelines on supply and property management.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to follow the guidelines of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) in the proper treatment and recognition of the mentioned accounts. Classify the accounts based on the nature and substance and accordingly adjust any accounting error resulting from the improper treatment.

Require the Municipal Accountant to book up the ₱ 2,000,000.00 financial subsidy granted to Don Carlos Polytechnic College (DCPC) in the books of accounts of the LGU.

Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to conduct monthly reconciliation regarding these matters in order to prevent possible occurrence of discrepancies at the same time they can be corrected straightaway, if there are any.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to follow the afore-cited provision. Regularly review the unexpended LDRRMF which are already more than five (5) years in the special trust fund. Consequently, the pertinent amounts should be reverted back to the general fund of the municipality so that it can be utilized by making it available for other social services as stipulated therein.

We recommended to require the Municipal Budget Officer to incorporate PPAs in accordance with the approved Annual Investment (AIP)/Development Plan pursuant to afore-cited provisions.  Improvement, concreting, perimeter fencing and multi-purpose building are not found in the context of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.  It is suggested also that the municipality shall reprogram/revise those disqualified projects and ensure that these are aligned with the aforementioned regulation and they are covered with a Supplemental Annual Investment Plan duly approved by the Sangguniang Bayan indicating the itemized programs, projects and activities.  Likewise, stop incurring additional expenditures for the Rehabilitation/Improvement of Public Cemetery under Social Services component and ensure timely submission of all budgets enacted so that incurrence of expenditures for inoperative appropriation(s), if any could be avoided.

We recommended making representation with DSWD thru the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to provide the LGU with a written guideline on the time frame regarding its submission of list of replacement and new pensioners whose amount of pensions is chargeable against the undisbursed release during a particular quarter.  This written guideline will serve as the basis of the Municipal Accountant to return the undisbursed amount.  In so doing payable accounts due to DSWD recorded in the LGU’s books of accounts will be reduced if not totally eradicated.

We recommended that the Ecological Solid Waste Management Program, in line with the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000, be followed accordingly in establishing proper disposal site. Further, we require the Municipal Ecological Solid Waste Management Coordinator to expedite the utilization of the budgeted Sanitary Landfill and Materials Recovery Facility.

Submit immediately the required documents to COA for complete technical review, and for succeeding projects submit Contracts and all its documents within five (5) days from the execution of contract for the timely review and verification of Contract and to avoid hassle in complying additional requirements for technical evaluation and to prevent future Audit Disallowance resulting from the conduct of technical review. 

•	Immediately submit to the Office of the Auditor all valid suspension/ Time Extension orders or any appropriate documents that would warrant the non-imposition of liquidated damages to the concerned contractor,  otherwise, direct the Municipal Accountant to compute for the liquidated damage to be collected from the contractors based on the provisions of Item 8, Annex E of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.  (Enclosed is checklist marked as Annex E of COA Circular NO. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009) and to submit a written consent of bondsmen per Section 10.5 of 2016 RIRR of R.A 9184. 
                                          

•	Monitor closely the implementation of the project to ensure that the terms and conditions of the contract are strictly complied and enforced, and to ensure the immediate completion of on-going project by reviewing thoroughly the Program of Work and evaluating project design and estimate to minimize variation orders and  time extensions  which often increase project cost.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to follow the guidelines of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) in the proper treatment and recognition of the mentioned accounts. Classify the accounts based on the nature and substance and accordingly adjust any accounting error resulting from the improper treatment.

Require the Municipal Accountant to book up the ₱ 2,000,000.00 financial subsidy granted to Don Carlos Polytechnic College (DCPC) in the books of accounts of the LGU.
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer to conduct monthly reconciliation regarding these matters in order to prevent possible occurrence of discrepancies at the same time they can be corrected straightaway, if there are any.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to follow the afore-cited provision. Regularly review the unexpended LDRRMF which are already more than five (5) years in the special trust fund. Consequently, the pertinent amounts should be reverted back to the general fund of the municipality so that it can be utilized by making it available for other social services as stipulated therein.

We recommended to require the Municipal Budget Officer to incorporate PPAs in accordance with the approved Annual Investment (AIP)/Development Plan pursuant to afore-cited provisions.  Improvement, concreting, perimeter fencing and multi-purpose building are not found in the context of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.  It is suggested also that the municipality shall reprogram/revise those disqualified projects and ensure that these are aligned with the aforementioned regulation and they are covered with a Supplemental Annual Investment Plan duly approved by the Sangguniang Bayan indicating the itemized programs, projects and activities.  Likewise, stop incurring additional expenditures for the Rehabilitation/Improvement of Public Cemetery under Social Services component and ensure timely submission of all budgets enacted so that incurrence of expenditures for inoperative appropriation(s), if any could be avoided.

We recommended making representation with DSWD thru the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Officer to provide the LGU with a written guideline on the time frame regarding its submission of list of replacement and new pensioners whose amount of pensions is chargeable against the undisbursed release during a particular quarter.  This written guideline will serve as the basis of the Municipal Accountant to return the undisbursed amount.  In so doing payable accounts due to DSWD recorded in the LGU’s books of accounts will be reduced if not totally eradicated.

Further, we require the Municipal Ecological Solid Waste Management Coordinator to expedite the utilization of the budgeted Sanitary Landfill and Materials Recovery Facility.

We recommended to require the Municipal Engineer to:
•	Submit immediately the required documents to COA for complete technical review, and for succeeding projects submit Contracts and all its documents within five (5) days from the execution of contract for the timely review and verification of Contract and to avoid hassle in complying additional requirements for technical evaluation and to prevent future Audit Disallowance resulting from the conduct of technical review.
•	Immediately submit to the Office of the Auditor all valid suspension/ Time Extension orders or any appropriate documents that would warrant the non-imposition of liquidated damages to the concerned contractor,  otherwise, direct the Municipal Accountant to compute for the liquidated damage to be collected from the contractors based on the provisions of Item 8, Annex E of the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.  (Enclosed is checklist marked as Annex E of COA Circular NO. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009) and to submit a written consent of bondsmen per Section 10.5 of 2016 RIRR of R.A 9184.
•	Monitor closely the implementation of the project to ensure that the terms and conditions of the contract are strictly complied and enforced, and to ensure the immediate completion of on-going project by reviewing thoroughly the Program of Work and evaluating project design and estimate to minimize variation orders and  time extensions  which often increase project cost.

We recommend to require the Municipal Engineer and Other Officers concerned for proper prioritization of fully funded projects to ensure their completion on its target set, thus precluding unwarranted delay of the implementation of programs, projects and activities.
Direct all the Accountable Officers to apply for fidelity bond and the amount of bond shall be in accordance with their maximum cash accountability.

Require the Municipal Engineer and Other Officers concerned for proper prioritization of fully funded projects to ensure their completion on its target set, thus precluding unwarranted delay of the implementation of programs, projects and activities.

Recommend the Local School Board (LSB) strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 7160 and the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars in the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget and identify the priority expenditures pursuant thereto. Any issues or conflicts arising from its implementation and cases not covered by the provisions shall be submitted to the DepEd for resolution and in consultation with the municipal government. Unrelated expenses like drugs and medicines may be charged to the General Fund of the municipality.

Observe the prescribed minimum employees’ monthly net take home pay of P3,500.00 under the GAA be observed. We also recommend that management stop immediately the practice of including the payments of loan amortizations to private lending institutions as deductions from the payroll and performing the collection and monitoring activities of the private lending institutions unless it is classified as a thrift bank or rural bank accredited by BSP. Also, require the personnel involved to transact directly with the private institutions.
We recommended management direct the Accountable Officer concerned to apply for fidelity bond and the amount of bond shall be in accordance with their maximum cash accountability.

We recommended the Local School Board (LSB) strictly adhere to the provisions of RA 7160 and the DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circulars in the preparation of the SEF Annual Budget and identify the priority expenditures pursuant thereto. Any issues or conflicts arising from its implementation and cases not covered by the provisions shall be submitted to the DepEd for resolution and in consultation with the municipal government. Unrelated expenses like drugs and medicines may be charged to the General Fund of the municipality.

We recommended management observe the prescribed minimum employees’ monthly net take home pay of P3,500.00 under the GAA be observed. We also recommend that management stop immediately the practice of including the payments of loan amortizations to private lending institutions as deductions from the payroll and performing the collection and monitoring activities of the private lending institutions unless it is classified as a thrift bank or rural bank accredited by BSP. Also, require the personnel involved to transact directly with the private institutions.

We recommended management require the Municipal Engineer and Other Officers concerned for proper prioritization of fully funded projects to ensure their completion on its target set, thus precluding unwarranted delay of the implementation of programs, projects and activities.
Implement strict observance of the reglementary period in the submission of financial reports and accounts to the Auditor to give him ample time for review.
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant to follow the said provision of the law.

Require the Municipal Engineer and Other Officers concerned for proper prioritization of fully funded projects to ensure their completion on its target set, thus precluding unwarranted delay of the implementation of programs, projects and activities.

Create an Inventory Team to conduct physical count of property, plant and equipment of the agency and require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the result of the account with the accounting records and to effect the necessary adjustments.

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to return the unutilized PDAF balance to the Bureau of Treasury/Department of Finance for reversion to the unappropriated surplus in the general fund of the national government in accordance with the Supreme Court decision dated November 19, 2013.

Open a special trust fund for unexpended LDRRMF to be utilized within the next five (5) years based on a separate LDRRMF Investment Plan. Submit monthly the Report on sources and Utilization of the DRRMF.
Require the Municipal Accountant adjust CIP accounts once the Municipal Engineer submit the list of completed and on-going projects and they should tally their records.

Instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Subsidiary Ledger and include in the Subsidiary of fixed Assets – Land Account, the complete description and all necessary information on each item of the acquired land.

Reconcile or Re-assess the Land Market Value submitted by the MASSO with the records of the Accounting Division.

Direct the officials and employees concerned to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel consumption to the auditor for review and evaluation. 

Indicate the License Plate Number of the motor vehicle on the withdrawal slip/charge invoice.

Require the Municipal Accountant to check/verify the entries made in the subsidiary ledger to establish the causes of the discrepancy / error and make the necessary correction to reconcile the accounts and to ensure correctness and accuracy on the financial statements.
